title: The Twenty-Second Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
## Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
## October 24, 2021 | Preacher: Richard Lawson

---

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Twenty-Second Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time, following the traditional liturgical calendar. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this date would have been Job 38:1-7 (27:1-6), Psalm 104:1-9, 25, 37c, Hebrews 5:1-10, and Mark 10:35-45—featuring Jesus's teaching about servanthood in response to James and John's request for positions of honor. The Episcopal Church was in the extended season of Ordinary Time, between Pentecost (June) and the beginning of Advent (late November).

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was navigating post-pandemic reopening and vaccination questions, with congregations across the country managing varying COVID-19 protocols. The Diocese of Colorado, under Bishop Kimberly Lucas (who began her tenure in 2020), was focused on reconciliation and inclusion initiatives following the national church's 2018 General Convention decisions on LGBTQ+ ordination and marriage. No specific major diocesan event is documented for this particular weekend, though Denver's cathedral as a prominent downtown church would have been managing significant attendance as in-person worship resumed more fully.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing unseasonably warm autumn weather in late October 2021, with highs in the 60s-70s Fahrenheit. Colorado was in the homestretch of the 2021 state legislative interim period, with various ballot measures and political campaigns gearing up for 2022. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, where Saint John's Cathedral is located, was experiencing ongoing downtown revitalization discussions and continuing management of homelessness and substance use issues that had intensified during the pandemic.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Biden administration was navigating ongoing supply chain disruptions affecting holiday shopping and inflation concerns dominating economic news. The trial of Gabby Petito's fiancé Brian Laundrie was in the national spotlight following her murder while traveling the country in a van. COVID-19 remained a significant story with vaccination debates ongoing, though case numbers were declining from the Delta variant surge; President Biden announced a vaccine mandate for federal contractors and healthcare workers facing legal challenges.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Taliban was consolidating control in Afghanistan following the August U.S. withdrawal, with the international community grappling with humanitarian crisis and refugee issues. Energy prices were surging across Europe heading into winter, with natural gas shortages becoming a political and economic crisis in the UK and Continental Europe. The COP26 climate conference was scheduled to begin in Glasgow, Scotland on October 31, 2021, generating significant pre-conference discussion about global climate commitments.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture was in a recovery and reconnection phase roughly 18 months into the pandemic, with cautious optimism mixed with persistent anxiety about health and economic stability. The mood was characterized by "return to normal" attempts alongside ongoing divisions over vaccines and masks—the pandemic remained a deeply polarizing touchstone. The Denver Broncos were in a disappointing 3-4 season, and Halloween was a week away, with American culture fully engaged in fall activities and holiday anticipation; pop culture was dominated by Marvel's "Eternals" heading toward November release and continued streaming entertainment consumption.
